name: SK Hynix
summary: SK Hynix is the world's #2 memory chip producer. **DAY 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; THREE-DAY window June 7 cutoff -> early June 10, June 8/9 runs empty; deltas vs June 7): SK HYNIX +~6% IN THE JUN 9 CHIP REBOUND — AI-ROTATION DRIVER, NOT A GULF SIGNAL; SECURED HELIUM INVENTORY STILL DEFUSES THE NEAR-TERM CLIFF.** SK Hynix gained +~6% on June 9 in the broad Korean chip rebound (NVIDIA-Korea AI-cooperation deals + President Lee's 'KOSPI is undervalued' remarks + bargain-hunting), as KOSPI rebounded ~4.8% after an >8% June 8 plunge. AI-rotation tape (post-Broadcom June 4 chip repricing), ~0% crisis, NOT a West Asia/Gulf signal. SK Hynix's secured helium inventory still DEFUSES the near-term cliff (the helium cascade is a LATENT multi-year risk, not today's driver) — the +~6% is sentiment/positioning, not input-supply. PORTFOLIO SIGNIFICANCE unchanged: SK Hynix (with Samsung) has been the SINK for FII capital leaving India; the bidirectional consecutive-day swings show a two-sided, volatile substitution trade, but the Jun 9 AI-deal rebound reasserts the bid (no confirmed sustained reversal). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: both triggers (supply-cut/halt; hyperscaler-financing) STAY watching. PRIOR — **DAY 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session [May jobs + AI rout] -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange): SK HYNIX -9% CONFIRMED IN THE 5-JUNE ASIA SESSION; ~-$12B FOREIGN NET-SELL SINCE MAY — REALISED VALUATION/POSITIONING UNWIND, NOT INPUT-SUPPLY.** The 5-June session realised the crack previewed 06-04: SK Hynix -9% (Samsung -7%, KOSPI -5.5% with a CIRCUIT BREAKER — KOSPI 200 futures -5%, worst KOSPI day in 19 months) as the crowded, leveraged North-Asia AI-memory trade unwound on the broad rates/AI risk-off (Broadcom AI-guidance miss + hot May jobs 172K vs ~85K -> sell-everything-rate-sensitive). Positioning is the tell: foreign investors net-sold ~$22B Korean equities since May, with SK Hynix alone ~-$12B. VALUATION/positioning (~0% crisis), NOT input-supply — SK Hynix states it has diversified/secured helium inventory (carry-forward CLAIM, one-sided), so the -9% is leveraged-trade de-risking, not a helium cliff. GRAPH HYGIENE: same -9% previewed 06-04 — confirm/refine, do NOT double-count. PORTFOLIO SIGNIFICANCE unchanged: SK Hynix (with Samsung) has been the SINK for FII capital leaving India — its realised valuation crack is hard evidence the capital_substitution trade can reverse toward India (markets C1/E1). One firm's helium claim is one-sided (CLAIMED); no production-halt announced; trigger stays watching. PRIOR — **DAY 97 (4 June): SK HYNIX -9% (NEAR A CIRCUIT-BREAKER) ON BROADCOM'S AI-GUIDANCE MISS — THE FIRST VALUATION CRACK IN THE SUPERCYCLE; AND SK HYNIX ITSELF SAID IT SECURED SUFFICIENT HELIUM INVENTORY, EASING THE NEAR-TERM KOREA CLIFF.** Broadcom's after-the-bell guide miss (AVGO -12.6%, SMH -4.4% live) cracked the crowded AI-memory trade: SK Hynix -9%, Samsung -7%, KOSPI -5.3%. This is VALUATION (~0% crisis), NOT helium — and notably SK Hynix STATED it has diversified helium sources and secured sufficient inventory (Tom's Hardware, intel A11), softening the most-cited near-term risk (Korean fabs running dry 'in June'). The structural shock persists (Ras Laffan ~30% of global helium offline since 28 Feb). PORTFOLIO SIGNIFICANCE: SK Hynix (with Samsung) has been the SINK for the FII capital leaving India — its first valuation crack is the first hard evidence the capital_substitution trade can reverse toward India (markets C1/E1). One firm's helium claim is one-sided (REPORTED); no production-halt announced; trigger stays watching. PRIOR — **DAY 96 (3 June): THE AI-MEMORY SUPERCYCLE STAYED DECOUPLED THROUGH THE FIRST CASUALTY DAY — KOSPI +0.1% / +31.5% MoM, SMH +26.1% MoM; Samsung + SK Hynix still ~half the KOSPI.** SK Hynix (with Samsung) is the destination of the FII capital leaving India, and the AI complex was untouched by the 3 June inflation-regime metals selloff. The live tail remains helium: Korea fab inventories estimated to last 'until June' (now INSIDE the buffer window); the capacity-zero / HBM-supercycle structural bid persists. Linde +2.3% on a broad-metals-selloff day is the cleanest helium-scarcity tell; NO SK Hynix/Samsung production-halt announced (trigger stays watching). PRIOR — **SK HYNIX HBM SHIPMENTS HITTING RECORD ON US HYPERSCALER DEMAND. SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX = 42.2% OF KOSPI WEIGHTING — INDEX MOVE IS THE CHIP RALLY. AI-CYCLE REGIME IS THE DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER FOR 2026. THE TRIPLE-STRESSOR STACK (HELIUM, SAMSUNG STRIKE, CAPACITY-ZERO) HAS BEEN SUBSTANTIALLY RELIEVED. SK HYNIX HBM4/NVIDIA PARTNERSHIP IS THE STRUCTURAL BID. AI-CYCLE REGIME IS THE DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER. SAMSUNG HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD FINAL TESTS WITH JUNE SUPPLY RAMP POSITIONED. KORNBLUTH 60-DAY PRODUCTION-CUT THESIS MATERIALLY WEAKENED.** Per markets dossier C2: Nvidia Q1 earnings released May 21 (profit +200%+ YoY, revenue +85% YoY) → chip-cycle bid revalidated. SK Hynix +9.5% Thursday on HBM4/Nvidia partnership; Samsung Electronics +7.5% on labor-strike aversion; TSMC +3%; KOSPI +~8% single-day Thursday, +22.8% 1M. **The triple-stressor stack (helium scarcity, Samsung strike, capacity-zero) has been substantially relieved** by HBM4 mitigation news. **Capacity-zero constraint persists** structurally — Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft/Nvidia/Amazon making unprecedented financing proposals. Nvidia took lion's share of 2026 output. Memory shortage expected to last through 2027 or 2030. **SK Hynix relative-strength play within memory complex**: brokerages maintained ₩3.2M targets. **Falsifiable**: SK Hynix or Samsung announces HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = Kornbluth thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified. PRIOR (May 22): **SK HYNIX Q1 CALL DISCLOSED 'SUFFICIENT HELIUM INVENTORY' + DIVERSIFIED SUPPLIERS (CNBC APRIL 23). SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX SECURED LONG-TERM US HELIUM CONTRACTS (DIGITIMES APRIL 9). THE KORNBLUTH 60-DAY PRODUCTION-CUT THESIS IS MATERIALLY CONSTRAINED.** SK Hynix said on its Q1 earnings call that it has 'diversified suppliers for key industrial inputs such as helium, bromine, and tungsten and has built up sufficient inventory, with limited impact expected on its production.' Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests with June supply ramp (Digitimes / Trendforce) — adjacent validation that the helium-mitigation channel is operational. **Capacity-zero constraint persists** — Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft/Nvidia/Amazon/Google making unprecedented financing proposals. **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** Memory shortage expected to last through 2027 or 2030. **SMH +2.1% 1D / +20.3% 1M / +40.4% 3M** on Nvidia Q1 earnings + helium-mitigation thesis. **Helium clock Day 7 of 14** — the immediate-60-day production-cut path Kornbluth identified is now the LESS likely branch; the mitigation-through-inventory-and-substitution path is the MORE LIKELY branch. **Falsifiable**: SK Hynix or Samsung announces HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = Kornbluth thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A10 SK HYNIX TRADES AT ₩1.836M (+0.93% Monday) — Brokerages (Eugene Investment, Mirae Asset) maintained ₩3.2M target prices and ₩10,000 KOSPI outlook; KOSPI +0.6% rally Monday confirms the Friday -6.1% break was the panic, not the trend.** Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits upside. **Helium clock Day 3 of 14 (TrendForce 'two-week clock'); ~11 days remain at full production rates per the March 24 framework. SK Hynix capacity-zero + Samsung 18-day strike May 21 + helium clock = three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** Samsung HBM4 announcement Monday is competitor-leadership signal but **SK Hynix maintains relative-strength position within memory complex** (brokerages reiterating ₩3.2M targets). **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — operative price is Friday May 15 close (SK Hynix -7.7%). Helium-clock-expiry window NOW DAY 2 OF 14.** **Samsung 18-day strike May 21 — 4 days from start, no new resolution development over the weekend.** Korean fab complex three-simultaneous-supply-side-stressors continue: helium scarcity, Samsung strike from May 21, capacity-zero. Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits the upside. **PRIOR (May 16):** SK Hynix is the world's #2 memory chip producer and the dominant HBM (high-bandwidth memory) supplier for AI accelerators. Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI weight. **JPMorgan estimates Samsung strike losses up to ₩43T ($28.8B); SK Hynix sits adjacent to the strike-window May 21-June 7 with capacity-zero constraint** — memory order-shift to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence; could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation but capacity-zero constraint limits upside. **Helium-clock-expiry window May 16-23 BEGINS TODAY** (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from May 16; ~8 days remain at full production rates per March 24 framework). **SK Hynix capacity-zero + Samsung 18-day strike + helium clock-expiry stack is now operationally synchronised — three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** PRIOR (May 15): **A9 SK HYNIX AVAILABLE CAPACITY 'ESSENTIALLY ZERO'** per insider reporting (CONFIRMED — Tom's Hardware, TradingKey, Wccftech, ad-hoc-news, Seoul Economic Daily, Bloomberg). Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon and Google making unprecedented financing proposals to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines (each running hundreds of millions of dollars). **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** SK Hynix is cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at potentially discounted prices could crimp future profitability. **Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030.** SK Hynix +144% YTD into May 14. SK Hynix has publicly claimed 'diversified supplies' on helium (CLAIMED — one-sided, NOT independently corroborated; TrendForce data-driven analysis is REPORTED). **Combined with the helium-clock-expiry window of May 16-23 and Samsung 18-day strike from May 21, SK Hynix sits at the center of the Korean fab complex's three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** **NEW TRIGGER**: 'SK Hynix announces supply guidance cut within 60 days' — would land helium-tsunami thesis. **Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron** flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could BENEFIT SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits the upside.
